title: Danbury, CT Engagement Contract for Corporation Counsel

communications.subject: Connecticut Freedom of Information Act Request: Danbury, CT Engagement Contract for Corporation Cousel
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Connecticut Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. The Engagement Contract of Daniel Casagrande and the firm of Cramer and Anderson for Corporation Counsel Services
2. Invoices for legal services for November and December 2025

I also request that, if possible and pursuant to Conn. Gen. Stat. § 1-212(d), fees be waived as I believe this request is in the public interest and not made for commercial gain. The requested documents will be made available to the public at MuckRock.com.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 4 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Kevin Paige

communications.communication:

-- 

Dianne E. Rosemark

City of Danbury 
203-797-4518

communications.subject: FOIA Request
communications.communication:
This will acknowledge receipt of your FOIA request received February 6,
2026, wherein you are seeking information pursuant to the
Connecticut Freedom of Information Act.



Please be advised that the City of Danbury is firmly committed to providing
prompt access to all records subject to disclosure under Connecticut
General Statutes §1-210, et. seq.  Accordingly, all documents will be
assembled and reviewed as quickly as reasonably possible in light of other
responsibilities and immediate commitments.  Although the City of Danbury
takes your request seriously and will respond as promptly as possible, we
reserve the right to petition the Freedom of Information Commission for a
declaratory ruling regarding requests that may be exempt from disclosure.



Please note, that there will be a charge of $.50 per page, and any amount
of over $10.00 must be paid in advance.



If you have any questions, please do not hesitate to contact our office.



[image: photo]
*Kimberly Anderson*
Legal Administrative Assistant
Office of Corporation Counsel
City of Danbury

(203) 797-4518 ext. 7212 | k.anderson@danbury-ct.gov

(203) 796-8043 Fax

155 Deer Hill Avenue, Danbury, CT 06810

Mon - Tue: 8:30 am - 6:00 pm

wed - Thurs: 9:00 am - 6:00 pm

*City Hall is closed on Fridays.*

title: Department of Justice Civil Rights Division First Amendment Cases

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Department of Justice Civil Rights Division First Amendment Cases
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

If the Department keeps a spreadsheet, database, or other tracking system of Civil Rights Division cases and investigations, I request a list (or a copy of a list) of cases opened since January 1, 2025 through November 1, 2025, where the case description or subject matter involves the First Amendment.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Adam Steinbaugh

communications.subject: FOIA Request 26-00043-F Final Response (ADB)
communications.communication:
ADB
Final Letter

[cid:image001.png@01DCAAF9.106D9D30]

U.S. Department of Justice
Civil Rights Division
Freedom of Information/PA Unit -4CON
950 Pennsylvania Ave., NW
Washington, DC 20530


Via Electronic Mail Only


Dear Mr. Steinbaugh,

This is in further response to your November 3, 2025  Freedom of Information Act/Privacy Act request seeking access to records pertaining to cases opened since January 1, 2025 through November 1, 2025, where the case description or subject matter involves the First Amendment.

After reviewing the responsive Civil Rights Division documents, I have determined that the attached document may be released to you in full.

If you are not satisfied with my response to this request, you may administratively appeal by writing to the Director, Office of Information Policy (OIP), United States Department of Justice, 441 G Street, NW, 6th Floor, Washington, DC 20530-0001, or you may submit an appeal through OIP's FOIAonline portal by creating an account on the following web site:  https://www.justice.gov/oip/submit-and-track-request-or-appeal.  Your appeal must be postmarked or electronically transmitted within 90 days of the date of my response to your request. If you submit your appeal by mail, both the letter and the envelope should be clearly marked "Freedom of Information Act Appeal." If possible, please provide a copy of your original request and this response letter with your appeal.

Additionally, you may contact the Office of Government Information Services (OGIS) at the National Archives and Records Administration to inquire about the FOIA medication services they offer. The contact information for OGIS is: Office of Government Information Services, National Archives and Records Administration, 8601 Adelphi Road - OGIS, College Park, Maryland 20740-6001; e-mail at ogis@nara.gov<mailto:ogis@nara.gov>; telephone at 202-741-5770; toll free at 1-877-684-6448; or facsimile at 202-741-5769. Please reference the FOIA/PA No. above in any correspondence with OGIS.

 I hope the Civil Rights Division has been of some assistance to you in this matter.

Sincerely,

Andrea D. Banks
Freedom of Information/Privacy Acts Unit
Civil Rights Division

title: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Wicomico County Sheriff)

communications.subject: Maryland Public Information Act Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Wicomico County Sheriff)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Maryland Public Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields or dates is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe

communications.subject: Re: Maryland Public Information Act Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Wicomico County Sheriff)
communications.communication:
Please be advised I no longer handle PIA requests for the Wicomico County Sheriff’s Office. I forwarded your original request to 1st Sgt Tony Glenn at tglenn@wicomicocounty.org for follow up. Please correspond with him at your earliest convenience.

Sincerely,
Lt Jessica Murphy

Get Outlook for iOS<https://aka.ms/o0ukef>

Mr. Sinyangwe,

This correspondence acknowledges receipt of your Maryland Public Information Act request submitted via MuckRock and received by the Wicomico County Sheriff’s Office on January 28, 2026, concerning use of force data for the period January 1, 2017, through December 31, 2025.

The Sheriff’s Office is currently unable to fully retrieve the requested data due to a software-related issue affecting the vendor-managed system from which the responsive records must be obtained. The vendor has been contacted and hopefully will have this resolved within thirty (30) days of the date your request was received.

Pursuant to § 4-203 of the Maryland General Provisions Article, the Sheriff’s Office is exercising its statutory authority to extend the response period where additional time is necessary to locate, retrieve, review, and prepare responsive records. This request remains under active review, and the delay is attributable to technical limitations beyond the immediate control of this office. This correspondence is not a denial of your request, nor a determination that responsive records do not exist.

Upon restoration of system access, the Sheriff’s Office will continue processing your request and will notify you of the availability of responsive records. You will also be advised in advance of any fees associated with the search, preparation, or reproduction of records, as required by law, before any release.

We appreciate your patience and understanding as we work to fulfill your request in compliance with the Maryland Public Information Act.

Sincerely,

1st Sgt. Anthony Glenn

communications.subject: RE: Maryland Public Information Act Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Wicomico County Sheriff)
communications.communication:
Dear Mr. Sinyanqwe,
I am writing in reference to your MPIA request to provide you with a status update. At this time, the system used to retrieve the requested information is experiencing technical issues, and we have been unable to access the records. We are actively working to resolve the issue and fulfill your request as soon as possible. If the information can be successfully retrieved, I am hopeful that we will be able to complete your request by the end of next week. I sincerely apologize for the delay and appreciate your patience and understanding. Unfortunately, the technical difficulties are beyond our control. Due to the delay, once the information is retrieved, all fees associated with researching and providing the records will be waived. You will not be obligated to pay any charges related to this request. Additionally, please note that Lt. Murphy no longer handles MPIA requests. Should you need to follow up or have any questions, feel free to contact me directly at tglenn@wicomicocounty.org<mailto:tglenn@wicomicocounty.org>.
I will promptly email you as soon as I am able to obtain the requested information.
Thank you again for your patience.

1st Sgt. Anthony Glenn
Division Commander, Administration
Wicomico County Sheriff’s Office
1661 Westwood Dr.
Salisbury Md. 21801
tglenn@wicomicocounty.org<mailto:tglenn@wicomicocounty.org>
410-548-4892 Ext. 243
[cid:image001.png@01DCA7E4.ED5C6630]
This e-mail message contains PRIVILEGED and CONFIDENTIAL information and is intended only for the use of the specific individual or individuals to which it is addressed. The information is intended for Law Enforcement use only.  If you are not an intended recipient of this e-mail, you are at this moment notified that any unauthorized use, dissemination or copying of this e-mail or the information contained herein or attached hereto is strictly prohibited. If you receive this e-mail in error, notify the person named above by reply e-mail and please delete it. Thank you

communications.subject: FW: Maryland Public Information Act Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Wicomico County Sheriff)
communications.communication:
Dear Mr. Sinyanqwe,
This correspondence is in reference to your request submitted pursuant to the Maryland Public Information Act (MPIA), Title 4 of the General Provisions Article of the Annotated Code of Maryland.
Our office is currently awaiting an update to our records management system. While this technical issue has affected the formatting of the data retrieved, it has not prevented us from accessing responsive records. In order to comply with the MPIA’s requirement to produce records within a reasonable time, we are providing the responsive documents at this time. Please note that due to the current system limitations, certain incidents may appear more than once under the same case number. Once the system update is completed, entries will be consolidated and reflected only once per associated case number.
Pursuant to § 4-206 of the General Provisions Article, although fees are typically assessed for the search, preparation, and reproduction of records, all fees associated with fulfilling this request are hereby waived due to the delay.
If you believe any portion of your request has been denied, in whole or in part, you have the right to seek review pursuant to § 4-1B-04 and § 4-362 of the General Provisions Article.
Should you have any questions or require clarification regarding the enclosed records, please do not hesitate to contact me.

1st Sgt. Anthony Glenn
Division Commander, Administration
Wicomico County Sheriff’s Office
1661 Westwood Dr.
Salisbury Md. 21801
tglenn@wicomicocounty.org<mailto:tglenn@wicomicocounty.org>
410-548-4892 Ext. 243

title: Traffic Arrests

communications.subject: Ohio Open Records Law Request: Traffic Arrests
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Ohio Open Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

Pursuant to the [State] Public Records Act, I request copies of police reports for all traffic stops conducted by the [Police Department] between August 1, 2025 and August 7, 2025 that resulted in an arrest. If no arrest please widen search from August 1, 2025 and August 31, 2025.

If the department cannot search by stop type, please instead provide:

The arrest log for the same dates; and The corresponding incident or arrest reports for those arrests that originated from a traffic stop.

Please provide these records in electronic format of PDF or Word/Excel. If any portion of the requested records is exempt from disclosure, please redact only the exempt material and release all reasonably segregable portions of the record.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Rights React

title: DOJ Voter Data Request Communications (California Secretary of State - Election Division)

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: DOJ Voter Data Request Communications (California Secretary of State - Election Division)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

From January 20, 2025 to the present:

1. All correspondence, emails, letters, and written communications between the agency and the U.S. Department of Justice, including but not limited to the Civil Rights Division, the Office of the Attorney General, and the Executive Office for Immigration Review, relating to voter registration data, voter files, voter rolls, or voter list maintenance.

2. All internal communications within the agency (including emails, memoranda, and briefing materials) discussing, referencing, or responsive to any DOJ request for voter registration data or any proposed or executed memorandum of understanding with the DOJ regarding voter data.

3. Any lists, spreadsheets, databases, or other records received from the DOJ identifying specific voters for removal, investigation, or further review.

4. All records relating to the engagement of any third-party contractor in connection with DOJ voter data requests or voter list maintenance activities conducted pursuant to any agreement with the DOJ.

If any records are withheld in whole or in part, please identify the specific statutory exemption relied upon for each withholding.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Audrey Nielsen

title: CPS LRE Distribution

communications.subject: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: CPS LRE Distribution
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records:

Case managers in local schools can run reports. CPS officials can also run reports on specific schools.

Please provide the following:

A report showing the LRE distribution in Courtenay Elementary school by homeroom.

A report showing the LRE distribution in Funston Elementary school by homeroom.

A report showing the LRE distribution in Mount Greenwood school by homeroom.

I am not looking for student specific information. I am only seeking Least Restrictive Environment information. This can be provided in aggregate or disaggregated. There should be a column listing Least Restrictive Environment by 1, 2 or 3. I do not want anonymized student IDs but one row of data should correspond to one student (if produced in disaggregated form). 

This request is made on behalf of The FOIA Bakery. Because this request is in the public interest for Illinois nonprofit The FOIA Bakery’s work to advocate for public education, please waive any fees in accordance with 5 ILCS § 140/6(c). Feel free to contact me with any questions, or if clarification is needed.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Miriam Bhimani

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Miriam Bhimani 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2218727/ 
 
Chicago Public Schools (CPS) responds to all public records requests in accordance with the Illinois Freedom of Information Act (FOIA), 5 ILCS 140/1 et seq. 
Due to the volume and complexity of requests received in the normal course of business, CPS is extending the time for its initial response to your request by an additional 5 business days (to 10 business days total beginning on the first business day following receipt of the request) pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/3(e)(v) which allows for the examination and evaluation of the requested records by personnel to determine if they are exempt from disclosure under Section 7 of Illinois FOIA or should be revealed only with appropriate deletions. 
Upon the completion of a response, CPS will provide the first 50 pages to you at no charge.  If a response is more than 50 pages, a fee of 15¢ per page will be charged for each additional page. All applicable fees must be paid prior to the copying. CPS will inform you of any fees due if your paper response exceeds 50 pages. You can monitor the progress of your request at the link below and you’ll receive an email when your request has been completed. Thank you for using the CPS FOIA Center.

communications.subject: Non-Commercial FOIA Request :: N019051-021626
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 
March 2, 2026 Miriam Bhimani RE: Chicago Public Schools FOIA Request N019051-021626 
Dear Miriam Bhimani: This letter is in reply to your Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) request seeking the following information: Case managers in local schools can run reports. CPS officials can also run reports on specific schools. 
Please provide the following: 
A report showing the LRE distribution in Courtenay Elementary school by homeroom. 
A report showing the LRE distribution in Funston Elementary school by homeroom. 
A report showing the LRE distribution in Mount Greenwood school by homeroom. RESPONSE: Records responsive to your FOIA request are available to download via the CPS FOIA Center. Please visit the link and enter your username and password to access the records. http://cps.edu/About_CPS/Departments/Law/Pages/FOIARequest.aspx . Under the rules of FOIA, identifiable student-level confidential information included within records are exempt from disclosure and have been redacted, including number of students where there are less than 10 students. Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act, section 7.5(r) exempts from inspection or copying information prohibited from being disclosed by the Illinois Student Records Act (ISSRA). ISSRA prohibits the disclosure of “school student records” and defines a “school student record” as “any writing or other recorded information concerning a student and by which a student may be individually identified, maintained by a school or at its direction or by an employee of a school, regardless of how or where the information is stored. 105 ILCS 10/2(d): Release of a minor’s record may only be authorized by the parent or guardian in accordance with the Illinois Student Records Act (105 ILCS 10/1 et seq.). To the extent you consider this a denial, pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/9.5(a) you may file a Request for Review with the Public Access Counselor (PAC) at: Public Access Counselor; Office of the Attorney General; 500 South 2nd Street; Springfield, IL 62706. You also have the right to seek judicial review of your denial by filing a lawsuit in the State circuit court, 5 ILCS 140/11. If you choose to file a Request for Review with the PAC, you must do so within 60 calendar days of the date of this denial letter. 5 ILCS 140/9.5(a). Please note that you must include a copy of your original FOIA request and this denial letter when filing a Request for Review with the PAC. 
Thank you for your request. Sincerely, 
Ana Diaz Freedom of Information Act Officer Chicago Public Schools

title: Financial Report Request (Middle Tennessee State University)

communications.subject: Tennessee Public Records Act Request: Financial Report Request (Middle Tennessee State University)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Tennessee Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1) I would like a copy of the annual NCAA MFRS report for FY2017 - FY2025. These annual reports are submitted by the athletic department and record the revenue/expenses of the department. It is an approximately 90 page document listing revenue and expenses in the athletic department by team. It is a standard annual NCAA form. The latest FY2025 report was due, 1/15/2025 so should be available. If you are unable to include this, can you please specify if you will send it to me immediately when it's available or if you want me to make a new request. That should be 9 reports combined.

2) I'd like copies of the latest contracts for your athletic director and head coaches of football, mens/womens basketball, and mens/womens ice hockey if you have those teams. That should be 6 documents if you have that many teams.

Please let me know if you have any questions. Thank you for your time.

Best,

Greg Chick

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 7 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Kent Hoover, a citizen of Tennessee, in coordination with Greg Chick.

title: Use of force data (2017-2024) (New Bedford Police Department)

communications.subject: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request: Use of force data (2017-2024) (New Bedford Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2024. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as we believe this request is in the public interest, as suggested but not stipulated by the recommendations of the Massachusetts Supervisor of Public Records. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, and is not made for commercial usage.

I expect the request to be filled in an accessible format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical documents.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe

If one or more of these fields is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. communications.subject: FDLE Office of General Counsel
communications.communication:
Thank you for contacting the Florida Department of Law Enforcement Office of General Counsel. This message is being provided as confirmation your message was received. You should receive a response to your inquiry as soon as possible.


If you need criminal history records, please visit our website, https://www.fdle.state.fl.us/Criminal-History-Records/Obtaining-Criminal-History-Information.aspx, and/or contact FDLE’s Criminal History Services Section at ApplicantChecks@fdle.state.fl.us<mailto:ApplicantChecks@fdle.state.fl.us> or (850) 410-8161.

Public records requests are processed in the order that they are received. Processing time may be lengthened due to the nature and extent of the request. Searches may be negative and FDLE may not have records responsive to your public records request. You may also receive an invoice for your request depending on the nature and extent of your public records request. Please feel free to contact us at (850) 410-7676, or publicrecords@fdle.state.fl.us, if you have additional specific information relating to the records you are requesting, as this may help expedite your request and/or reduce costs that may be associated with the request.

Additional information regarding how to obtain public records including what costs may be applicable is contained from the hyperlink below. We are committed to our fundamental values of Service, Integrity, Respect, and Quality.

For Additional Information click http://www.fdle.state.fl.us/OGC/Public-Records.aspx<https://www.fdle.state.fl.us/OGC/Public-Records.aspx>

communications.subject: FW: Florida Sunshine Law Request: Guidance to Florida universities and colleges on antisemitism offenses (Department of Law Enforcement) PRR-2023-3333
communications.communication:
RE:      Your Public Records Request Tracking number, FDLE Docket No. PRR-2023-3333
Requesting public records regarding the memo providing “detailed guidance on these laws” as referenced in the October 17, 2023, letter from Attorney General Ashley Moody to “All Florida Colleges and Universities and All Florida College and University Campus Police Chiefs.”

Hello Adam,

Your public records request is being processed and we are notifying you of your tracking number for this request.

Please be advised that the Florida Department of Law Enforcement (FDLE) is not a college or university, and we are not part of the Florida Attorney General’s Office.  You may wish to submit your public records request to those records custodians as well.

Please feel free to contact us if you have additional specific information relating to the records you are requesting, as this may help expedite your request and/or reduce costs that may be associated with the request. If you no longer need these records please let our office know.

Public records requests are processed in the order that they are received. Processing time may be lengthy due to the nature and extent of the request. Searches may be negative and FDLE may not have records responsive to your public records request. You may also receive an invoice for your request depending on the nature and extent of your public records request. A copy of the FDLE Guide to Public Records is attached for your review.

Please be advised that FDLE may not be the custodian of all of the records you requested. FDLE is not the custodian of all State of Florida criminal incident-related records for every state and local criminal justice agency.

Additional information regarding how to obtain public records including what costs may be applicable may be found on our website http://www.fdle.state.fl.us/OGC/Public-Records.aspx.
We are committed to our fundamental values of Service, Integrity, Respect, and Quality.

If you have questions concerning your request, please contact us at (850) 410-7676 or publicrecords@fdle.state.fl.us<mailto:publicrecords@fdle.state.fl.us>, and please include your “PRR” docket number we’ve provided above.

Sincerely,

Office of General Counsel
Florida Department of Law Enforcement
Post Office Box 1489
Tallahassee, FL 32302-1489

CONFIDENTIALITY NOTICE - The information contained in this e-mail transmission from the Florida Department of Law Enforcement is privileged and CONFIDENTIAL. It is intended for the sole use of the person (s) or entity named. If you are not the intended recipient of this transmission, the dissemination, distribution, copying, or other use of this information may constitute a violation of §119, F. S. and is strictly prohibited. If you have received this email in error, please contact the sender immediately.

title: Law Enforcement Agencies Accessing the North Dakota Bureau of Criminal Investigation's Face Recognition System

communications.subject: North Dakota Open Records Law Request: Law Enforcement Agencies Accessing the North Dakota Bureau of Criminal Investigation's Face Recognition System
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the North Dakota Open Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

I would like to learn: 1) which local, state and national law enforcement agencies can currently submit probe photos to be run through the Bureau of Criminal Investigation's face recognition system, and 2) which law enforcement agencies actually submitted probe photos to the Bureau for a search from Jan. 1, 2025 to the date of the most recently available data.

Please provide any lists, logs or datasets showing this information.  With regard to agencies that have submitted probe photos, please provide logs, lists or datasets describing, as available, the requesting organization, the number of requests made, the dates of the requests, and the number of requests granted.  If the Bureau maintains this information in an Excel spreadsheet, I request that the record be produced in this format.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Dave Maass

communications.subject: RE: North Dakota Open Records Law Request: Law Enforcement Agencies Accessing the North Dakota Bureau of Criminal Investigation's Face Recognition System
communications.communication:
Good Afternoon Mr. Maass,

We have received your records request. Under the BCI's facial recognition practice, law enforcement agencies may request a facial recognition search if the request is made for a valid law enforcement purpose, is supported by a criminal predicate, and includes a case or intelligence number.

Attached is a spreadsheet listing the agencies that submitted probe photo requests between January 1, 2025, and the present, including the date of each request and the number of requests submitted on that date.

The Bureau does not maintain a separate log reflecting whether requests were granted or denied. Accordingly, no responsive records exist for that portion of your request.

Respectfully,

Chief Agent Casey Miller
North Dakota Bureau of Criminal Investigation
[cid:image001.png@01DCAA48.95DA9650]

communications.communication:
RE: PUBLIC RECORDS REQUEST # R011217-011626 
 
 
Dear Ileana Garnand, 
 
The University of Texas at Austin received a public information request from you which provides as follows: 
 To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Texas Public Information Act, I hereby request the following records: 

Communications and correspondence between the University of Texas at Austin and the federal government about the 'Compact for Academic Excellence in Higher Education' sent to the school in October 2025. 

I additionally request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as I believe this request is in the public interest. Sincerely, 

Ileana Garnand 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2176029/ 
 
Section 552.222 of the Texas Public Information Act permits a governmental body to ask a requestor to clarify or narrow a request for information if the request is unclear or if a large amount of information has been requested. The purpose of this letter is to ask that you clarify or narrow your request by providing us with more information about the particular records you seek. The following paragraph highlights the aspect of your request that we find to be unclear or overly broad. 
 
We are not able to centrally perform a University-wide search for communications. Please specify which University department or employee whose records we should search for responsive information. 
 
Please let us know how you wish to proceed. Upon receipt of your response, we will resume work on your request.  You may deliver your response by replying to this email or by any one of the following means: 
 
By U.S. Mail: 
Attn:  Open Records Coordinator 
Office of the Vice President for Legal Affairs 
The University of Texas at Austin 
P.O. Box R 
Austin, Texas 78713-8918 
By Hand Delivery: 
Office of the Vice President for Legal Affairs 
Peter T. Flawn Academic Center, Suite 438 
2304 Whitis Avenue 
Austin, Texas 78712 
By Fax:  
512-471-1255 
Please note that, in accordance with section 552.222 of the Texas Government Code, your request will be considered withdrawn if you do not respond to this request for narrowing and clarification in writing within 61 days from the date of this letter. 
If you have any questions, please contact my office at 512-471-8300. Thank you for your attention. 
Sincerely, 
 
 
Bob Bonner-Davis 
Chief Public Information Coordinator 
Office of the Vice President for Legal Affairs

title: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (San Juan County Sheriff)

communications.subject: New Mexico Inspection of Public Records Act Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (San Juan County Sheriff)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New Mexico Inspection of Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields or dates is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 15 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe

communications.communication:
Hello!

As these are two separate FOIA requests I will only be responding to "Flock Safety Emails Dec 2023 - Feb 2026".

As for this request here is a narrowed request made under the provisions of Illinois Freedom of Information Act. for the following records:

Copies of all emails received by your agency from any sender email address containing the domain @flocksafety.com (**but excluding emails from <hotlist@flocksafety.com>) between 10/1/2025 and the date this request is processed (inclusive of those dates). 

This drastically limits the scope of search from 25 months to 4 months.

This request includes but is not limited to:

1. Emails received by any employee official or representative of Sterling Police Department using an agency issued email account 

2. All attachments included with those emails 

3. Related metadata sufficient to identify sender recipient (To:, CC:, BCC:), date and time of receipt, and subject line.

This can likely be done by searching "@flocksafety.com" in your search, and limiting or redacting any with "hotlist" at the beginning. 

And as for the "Flock Safety ALPR Audits 2026 Q1 (Sterling Police Department Illinois)" request, I am happy to talk about that in its proper channel.

With thanks,
 
Zeke Martin

title: Austin bombings / Mark Anthony Conditt

communications.subject: Texas Public Information Act Request: Austin bombings / Mark Anthony Conditt
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Texas Public Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

In 2018 a series of bombings were perpetuated by Mark Anthony Conditt. The investigation involved the FBI, the Austin Police Department, and the Texas Department of Public Safety. Austin Police Department is referenced as the leading agency by the FBI in publicly released documents which state “with the Austin Police Department (APD) as the lead law enforcement agency o n the case, the FBI worked together with the Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms and Explosives (ATF), the U.S. Postal Service and the Texas Department of Public Safety (DPS) to support the operation from a joint command center at the Combined Transportation Emergency Communications Center (CTECC), located in Austin, Texas.” (https://vault.fbi.gov/austin-bombings/Austin%20Bombings%20Part%2001%20(Final)/view p47 ) Additionally, the Texas Department of Public Safety has referred me to your department after they have claimed not to be in possession of responsive documents. (https://www.muckrock.com/foi/texas-109/mark-anthony-conditt-and-austin-bombing-texas-department-of-public-safety-204697/) 

I am requesting the following items:

- Audio or direct transcription of the full 25 minute “confession” video recording by Mark Anthony Conditt recovered by law enforcement after his death. The existence of this tape is public knowledge and has been publicly documented by Governor Greg Abbot, former Austin Police chief Brian Manley, and internal FBI files  -

     https://web.archive.org/web/20180323120234/https://www.statesman.com/news/breaking-austin-bombing-suspect-dies-police-close-official-says/KZmUAGvKlNazDr31EzeUzI/

     https://www.washingtonpost.com/news/morning-mix/wp/2018/03/22/austin-bomber-challenged-young-man-or-terrorist/
     
     https://vault.fbi.gov/austin-bombings/Austin%20Bombings%20Part%2001%20(Final)/view (p40)

- Any documents relating to the investigation, identification, or pursuit of Mark Anthony Conditt

- Any documents regarding a follow up investigation on any groups or associates of Mark Anthony Conditt.

I ask that the fee be waived as this information is within the public interest and many documents were widely commented on despite them not being available to the public (namely the 25 minute recording) release and access of this information is crucial to fair and accurate reporting on this matter.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Nate Zaffiro

communications.subject: Public Information Act Request :: R179586-022226
communications.communication:
Dear Nate Zaffiro: 
 
Thank you for your interest in the public records of the Austin Police Department. Your request has been received and is being processed. Your request was received in this office on 2/22/2026 and given the reference number R179586-022226 for tracking purposes. 
Please be advised that the Austin Police Department’s Central Records Division receives over 2500 requests for information a month. These requests are processed in the order received, and we strive to respond to each request promptly, as required by law. 
Please know that, as a result of the large volume of requests received, the anticipated timeframe for responding to your request may take 10 business days if it is simply an offense report you're requesting.  If you are requesting body cam, dash cam, or other related records your request could take up to 30 days. 
You will be contacted about the availability and/or provided with copies of the records requested. PLEASE NOTE: The Texas Public Information Act does not require a governmental body to create new information, to do legal research, or to answer questions. 
You can monitor the progress of your request at the link below and you’ll receive an email when your request has been completed. Again, thank you for using the Austin Public Records Center. 
Austin Police Department

In 2018 a series of bombings were perpetuated by Mark Anthony Conditt. The investigation involved the FBI, the Austin Police Department, and the Texas Department of Public Safety. Austin Police Department is referenced as the leading agency by the FBI in publicly released documents which state “with the Austin Police Department (APD) as the lead law enforcement agency o n the case, the FBI worked together with the Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms and Explosives (ATF), the U.S. Postal Service and the Texas Department of Public Safety (DPS) to support the operation from a joint command center at the Combined Transportation Emergency Communications Center (CTECC), located in Austin, Texas.” (https://vault.fbi.gov/austin-bombings/Austin%20Bombings%20Part%2001%20(Final)/view p47 ) Additionally, the Texas Department of Public Safety has referred me to your department after they have claimed not to be in possession of responsive documents. The existence of this tape is public knowledge and has been publicly documented by Governor Greg Abbot, former Austin Police chief Brian Manley, and internal FBI files - 

https://web.archive.org/web/20180323120234/https://www.statesman.com/news/breaking-austin-bombing-suspect-dies-police-close-official-says/KZmUAGvKlNazDr31EzeUzI/ 

https://www.washingtonpost.com/news/morning-mix/wp/2018/03/22/austin-bomber-challenged-young-man-or-terrorist/

I ask that the fee be waived as this information is within the public interest and many documents were widely commented on despite them not being available to the public (namely the 25 minute recording) release and access of this information is crucial to fair and accurate reporting on this matter. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Nate Zaffiro 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2224293/” 
APD PIO has received your request and will work to process the information requested.  As the process begins, we are clear you are willing to exclude all records and information that maybe considered confidential by law, such as an open investigation, pending prosecution or common-law privacy. 
Please be advised the 10-day response rule does not require the records released within 10 days.  Please know this is your 10 day response.  In accordance with the Public Information Act, Chapter 552 of the Texas Government Code, the City of Austin, TX makes all of its public records available to citizens. 
Please be advised that the Austin Police Department’s Central Records Division receives over 2,500 requests for information a month. These requests are processed in the order received, and we strive to respond to each request promptly, as required by law. 
The Texas Public Information Act requires governmental bodies to provide access to existing public information and does not impose an obligation to answer questions, explain legal reasoning, justify policy decisions, or create new records or analyses. See Tex. Gov’t Code §§ 552.002, 552.021, 552.028. The Texas Attorney General has expressly recognized that the Act is not intended to function as a discovery statute. See Open Records Decision No. 452 (1986). Should your correspondence seek explanations, legal conclusions, or policy justification beyond the production of existing public information, such matters fall outside the scope of the Texas Public Information Act and do not require further response. 
Please know that, as a result of the large volume of requests received, the anticipated timeframe for responding to your request is approximately 45-60 business days. 
For more information on the Public Information Act, please visit the Texas Attorney General website. 

Also, please know APD does not upload any records to links or open links supplied, as we practice internet security and safety rules. 
Sincerely, 
Open Records Unit 
Austin Police Department

https://web.archive.org/web/20180323120234/https://www.statesman.com/news/breaking-austin-bombing-suspect-dies-police-close-official-says/KZmUAGvKlNazDr31EzeUzI/ 

https://www.washingtonpost.com/news/morning-mix/wp/2018/03/22/austin-bomber-challenged-young-man-or-terrorist/ 

https://vault.fbi.gov/austin-bombings/Austin%20Bombings%20Part%2001%20(Final)/view (p40) 

- Any documents relating to the investigation, identification, or pursuit of Mark Anthony Conditt 

- Any documents regarding a follow up investigation on any groups or associates of Mark Anthony Conditt. 

I ask that the fee be waived as this information is within the public interest and many documents were widely commented on despite them not being available to the public (namely the 25 minute recording) release and access of this information is crucial to fair and accurate reporting on this matter. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Nate Zaffiro 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2224293/” 
APD PIO has received your request and will work to process the information requested.  As the process begins, we are clear you are willing to exclude all records and information that maybe considered confidential by law, such as an open investigation, pending prosecution or common-law privacy. 
Please be advised the 10-day response rule does not require the records released within 10 days.  Please know this is your 10 day response.  In accordance with the Public Information Act, Chapter 552 of the Texas Government Code, the City of Austin, TX makes all of its public records available to citizens. 
Please be advised that the Austin Police Department’s Central Records Division receives over 2,500 requests for information a month. These requests are processed in the order received, and we strive to respond to each request promptly, as required by law. 
The Texas Public Information Act requires governmental bodies to provide access to existing public information and does not impose an obligation to answer questions, explain legal reasoning, justify policy decisions, or create new records or analyses. See Tex. Gov’t Code §§ 552.002, 552.021, 552.028. The Texas Attorney General has expressly recognized that the Act is not intended to function as a discovery statute. See Open Records Decision No. 452 (1986). Should your correspondence seek explanations, legal conclusions, or policy justification beyond the production of existing public information, such matters fall outside the scope of the Texas Public Information Act and do not require further response. 
Please know that, as a result of the large volume of requests received, the anticipated timeframe for responding to your request is approximately 45-60 business days. 
For more information on the Public Information Act, please visit the Texas Attorney General website. 

Also, please know APD does not upload any records to links or open links supplied, as we practice internet security and safety rules. 
Sincerely, 
Open Records Unit 
Austin Police Department

communications.subject: [Austin Public Records Center] :: R179586-022226
communications.communication:

Re: Open Record Request of February 22, 2026, Reference # R179586-022226 
Dear Nate Zaffiro, 
The Austin Police Department (APD) received a Public Information request from you on February 22, 2026, to request copies of records pertaining to the following: “To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Texas Public Information Act, I hereby request the following records: 

In 2018 a series of bombings were perpetuated by Mark Anthony Conditt. The investigation involved the FBI, the Austin Police Department, and the Texas Department of Public Safety. Austin Police Department is referenced as the leading agency by the FBI in publicly released documents which state “with the Austin Police Department (APD) as the lead law enforcement agency o n the case, the FBI worked together with the Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms and Explosives (ATF), the U.S. Postal Service and the Texas Department of Public Safety (DPS) to support the operation from a joint command center at the Combined Transportation Emergency Communications Center (CTECC), located in Austin, Texas.” (https://vault.fbi.gov/austin-bombings/Austin%20Bombings%20Part%2001%20(Final)/view p47 ) Additionally, the Texas Department of Public Safety has referred me to your department after they have claimed not to be in possession of responsive documents. (https://www.muckrock.com/foi/texas-109/mark-anthony-conditt-and-austin-bombing-texas-department-of-public-safety-204697/) 

I am requesting the following items: 

- Audio or direct transcription of the full 25 minute “confession” video recording by Mark Anthony Conditt recovered by law enforcement after his death. The existence of this tape is public knowledge and has been publicly documented by Governor Greg Abbot, former Austin Police chief Brian Manley, and internal FBI files - 

https://web.archive.org/web/20180323120234/https://www.statesman.com/news/breaking-austin-bombing-suspect-dies-police-close-official-says/KZmUAGvKlNazDr31EzeUzI/ 

https://www.washingtonpost.com/news/morning-mix/wp/2018/03/22/austin-bomber-challenged-young-man-or-terrorist/ 

https://vault.fbi.gov/austin-bombings/Austin%20Bombings%20Part%2001%20(Final)/view (p40) 

- Any documents relating to the investigation, identification, or pursuit of Mark Anthony Conditt 

- Any documents regarding a follow up investigation on any groups or associates of Mark Anthony Conditt. 

I ask that the fee be waived as this information is within the public interest and many documents were widely commented on despite them not being available to the public (namely the 25 minute recording) release and access of this information is crucial to fair and accurate reporting on this matter. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Nate Zaffiro 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2224293/” 
In order for APD to begin processing your request, we need additional clarification. 
With regard to your request seeking:  "- Any documents relating to the investigation, identification, or pursuit of Mark Anthony Conditt 

- Any documents regarding a follow up investigation on any groups or associates of Mark Anthony Conditt." 
This is overbroad. Please narrow your search to certain records as this request might result in a substantial cost time estimate, if not narrowed. 

 

APD has considered your request for a public-interest cost waiver pursuant to Texas Government Code § 552.267(a). APD evaluates fee-waiver requests on a case-by-case basis to determine whether waiver of charges would primarily benefit the general public. 
APD has determined that a cost waiver is not warranted for this request. Processing the requested records requires staff time to locate, gather, compile, and review responsive information maintained across multiple custodial units, including individualized review for information made confidential by law. The labor associated with these tasks is chargeable under Subchapter F of the Texas Public Information Act, and waiver of such charges would not primarily benefit the general public within the meaning of § 552.267(a). 
To reduce costs, APD has offered the option to narrow or clarify the scope of the request. APD will proceed with issuance of a lawful, itemized cost estimate consistent with the Texas Public Information Act. 
If you would like for the APD to proceed with your public information request, please provide clarifications within sixty-one (61) calendar days from 02/24/2026. Otherwise, the APD will consider your request closed/withdrawn. 
You may provide a response to the APD by any of the following methods:  
  * by responding to this email or 
  * by regular mail to Austin Police Department Attn: Open Records, P.O. Box 689001, Austin, TX  78768-9001 
Upon receipt of your clarification, the APD will further respond to your Public Information Act Request as required by law, including providing you with an estimate of costs associated with producing the requested records. 
Thank you for contacting the Austin Police Department. 
Austin Police Department

title: National Institute of Diabetes legal name documents

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: National Institute of Diabetes legal name documents
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

The March 13 memo informing institute employees that they can only use “full legal name” in email signatures and cannot use pronouns or "nicknames". I also request any other agency memos, guidance documents or communications regarding this new policy or any other policy implemented in response to Executive Order 14168, known as "Defending Women from Gender Ideology Extremism and Restoring Biological Truth to the Federal Government."

I request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as I believe this request is in the public interest. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, processed by a representative of the news media/press and is made in the process of news gathering and not for commercial usage.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Ileana Garnand

communications.subject: RE: [EXTERNAL] Freedom of Information Act Request: National Institute of Diabetes legal name documents
communications.communication:
Good afternoon Ileana,

We are in receipt of your FOIA request below and need more information/clarification before we can begin processing your request. First, is your request specific to the National Institute of Diabetes & Digestive & Kidney Diseases (NIDDK) or all of NIH? It mentions NIDDK at the top but we just want to make sure we are searching the correct offices for the documents you have requested. Also, it would be helpful to have direct contact information (email, phone number, address) because we have run into issues in the past with getting communications to requesters through the MuckRock platform.

Thank you,
NHLBI FOIA Service Center

title: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Washington County Sheriff)

communications.subject: Minnesota Government Data Practices Act Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Washington County Sheriff)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Minnesota Government Data Practices Act, I hereby request the following records:

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields or dates is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe

title: CPRA Request: Flock Safety ALPR Records

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: CPRA Request: Flock Safety ALPR Records
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Unless a different timeframe is specified below, this request covers records from January 1, 2024, to the date of this request.

I. ALPR Data Intake from Specified Agencies
1. All records identifying the law enforcement agencies that currently transmit, upload, or otherwise provide Automated License Plate Reader (ALPR) data to NCRIC, including the method of transmission for each agency (e.g., automatic real-time feed, batch upload, or query-based access).
2. All Memoranda of Understanding (MOUs), data-sharing agreements, network-participation terms, or other written agreements between NCRIC and the following agencies governing the sharing or receipt of ALPR data:
a. San Mateo County Sheriff's Office (SMCSO)
b. Belmont Police Department (Belmont, California)
c. Burlingame Police Department (Burlingame, California)
d. Daly City Police Department (Daly City, California)
e. San Bruno Police Department (San Bruno, California)
3. Records identifying the volume of ALPR plate reads received by NCRIC from each of the five agencies listed in Item 2, aggregated by month.
4. Records identifying the ALPR technology vendors or platforms (e.g., Flock Safety, Vigilant Solutions/Motorola, Rekor, etc.) from which NCRIC receives or ingests data, and whether the data from each of the five agencies listed in Item 2 arrives through a vendor-operated platform or through a direct agency feed.

II. Query and Access Logs
5. All existing audit or access logs generated by NCRIC's ALPR system(s) documenting queries or searches of ALPR data originating from each of the following agencies, with results provided separately for each agency:
a. San Mateo County Sheriff's Office
b. Belmont Police Department
c. Burlingame Police Department
d. Daly City Police Department
e. San Bruno Police Department
For each query record, I request: the date and time of the query, the agency or unit of the individual conducting the query, and the stated purpose or justification for the query as recorded in the system. I request that personally identifiable information of non-employee query subjects be redacted rather than the records withheld in their entirety.
6. Records identifying the total number of queries made against NCRIC's ALPR database(s) by all users, aggregated by month, and broken down by querying agency where such records exist.
7. A list of all agencies, units, or task forces (local, state, and federal) whose personnel currently have authorized access to query NCRIC's ALPR database(s).

III. Downstream Sharing and Federal Access
8. All records documenting instances in which ALPR data held by NCRIC (including data originating from any of the five agencies listed in Item 2) was provided to, accessed by, or queried by personnel of any federal agency, including but not limited to:
a. U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE)
b. Homeland Security Investigations (HSI)
c. U.S. Customs and Border Protection (CBP)
d. Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI)
e. Drug Enforcement Administration (DEA)
f. Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms and Explosives (ATF)
g. U.S. Marshals Service
9. All records documenting instances in which intelligence products, analytical reports, or investigative leads derived in whole or in part from ALPR data were disseminated by NCRIC to any federal agency or to personnel assigned to a federal task force, including joint task forces operating under federal direction.
10. Any policies, directives, or written guidance that govern or restrict the downstream dissemination of ALPR data or ALPR-derived intelligence by NCRIC to federal agencies, including any restrictions imposed by the originating local agency (e.g., any of the five agencies listed in Item 2).
11. Any policies or directives addressing NCRIC's obligations under California's sanctuary or data-privacy laws (including the California Values Act, SB 54, and Civil Code § 1798.90.51 et seq.) as they relate to the sharing of ALPR data with federal immigration enforcement agencies.

IV. Policy, Retention, and Oversight
12. NCRIC's current ALPR Usage and Privacy Policy, as required by California Civil Code § 1798.90.51, including data retention schedules.
13. All annual audit reports of NCRIC's ALPR program required by California Civil Code § 1798.90.53, from January 1, 2023, to the present.
14. Records identifying the current data retention period for ALPR data held by NCRIC, and any records reflecting changes to the retention period during the timeframe of this request.
15. Records of any unauthorized access, data breaches, policy violations, or security incidents involving ALPR data held by NCRIC.
16. Records of any complaints or inquiries received from members of the public, advocacy organizations, or elected officials regarding NCRIC's ALPR data practices.

V. Hot Lists and Alerting
17. A list of all categories of "hot lists" (e.g., stolen vehicles, AMBER alerts, BOLO lists) currently integrated into or compared against NCRIC's ALPR database(s), including the source of each hot list.
18. Any records describing the use of custom or locally generated hot lists, including the criteria or authorization process for creating such lists.

VI. Governance and Oversight Structure
19. The names and titles of NCRIC personnel responsible for administering, overseeing, or auditing the ALPR program.
20. Agendas, minutes, or summaries of any meetings of NCRIC's Privacy, Civil Rights, and Civil Liberties Advisory Committee (or equivalent oversight body) at which ALPR data practices were discussed, from January 1, 2023, to the present.

If you determine that any portion of the requested records is exempt from disclosure, I ask that you redact only those portions and provide the remainder of the record. For any redaction or denial, please cite the specific legal exemption justifying the withholding, as required by Government Code § 7922.540.

Given that this request is made in the public interest by a student journalist writing for Scot Scoop, I respectfully request a waiver or reduction of any fees associated with this request.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Maximus Kwan

communications.subject: Re: California Public Records Act Request: CPRA Request: Flock Safety ALPR Records
communications.communication:
Note: the Request uses terms that imply large-scale data movement or duplication such as "intake" and "ingest" and "receive" and "transmit" and "upload" and "held" when it should be clarified that NCRIC only has ad-hoc query-based access to ALPR data hosted within the Flock and Vigilant platforms, through standard browser-based interfaces provided by the vendor, with the breadth of potential matches of those queries limited by the sharing rules, permissions, and retention policies established and directly controlled by the other agencies who share their data.

  1.  All records identifying the law enforcement agencies that currently transmit, upload, or otherwise provide Automated License Plate Reader (ALPR) data to NCRIC, including the method of transmission for each agency (e.g., automatic real-time feed, batch upload, or query-based access). See attached "Agencies sharing their Flock ALPR data with NCRIC"
  2.  All Memoranda of Understanding (MOUs), data-sharing agreements, network-participation terms, or other written agreements between NCRIC and the following agencies governing the sharing or receipt of ALPR data. See attached data sharing Memorandum of Understandings and NCRIC ALPR Policy.
  3.  Records identifying the volume of ALPR plate reads received by NCRIC from each of the five agencies listed in Item 2, aggregated by month. No such records exist. NCRIC has no visibility into the activity of other agency/customer ALPR devices. NCRIC receives no ALPR data from other agencies. NCRIC only has query-based access to ALPR data in Flock where the Flock customer agency has elected to allow access.
  4.  Records identifying the ALPR technology vendors or platforms (e.g., Flock Safety, Vigilant Solutions/Motorola, Rekor, etc.) from which NCRIC receives or ingests data, and whether the data from each of the five agencies listed in Item 2 arrives through a vendor-operated platform or through a direct agency feed. NCRIC has ad-hoc query-based access to ALPR data in Flock and Vigilant through standard vendor-operated interfaces.
  5.  All existing audit or access logs generated by NCRIC's ALPR system(s) documenting queries or searches of ALPR data originating from each of the following agencies, with results provided separately for each agency. It is unclear whether this request is for queries/searches conducted by users from the listed agencies, or searches that may have included data from the listed agencies. For either, that information may be available from the agency but NCRIC does not have records responsive to this request.
  6.  Records identifying the total number of queries made against NCRIC's ALPR database(s) by all users, aggregated by month, and broken down by querying agency where such records exist. No such summary report exists.
  7.  A list of all agencies, units, or task forces (local, state, and federal) whose personnel currently have authorized access to query NCRIC's ALPR database(s). See attached "Agencies NCRIC shares its Flock ALPR data with" for other Flock customer agencies granted searchability to our records.
  8.  All records documenting instances in which ALPR data held by NCRIC (including data originating from any of the five agencies listed in Item 2) was provided to, accessed by, or queried by personnel of any federal agency, including but not limited to...No such records exist. NCRIC does not share ALPR data or provide access to ALPR systems to any federal personnel.
  9.  All records documenting instances in which intelligence products, analytical reports, or investigative leads derived in whole or in part from ALPR data were disseminated by NCRIC to any federal agency or to personnel assigned to a federal task force, including joint task forces operating under federal direction. No such records exist. NCRIC does not share ALPR data or provide access to ALPR systems to any federal personnel.
  10. Any policies, directives, or written guidance that govern or restrict the downstream dissemination of ALPR data or ALPR-derived intelligence by NCRIC to federal agencies, including any restrictions imposed by the originating local agency (e.g., any of the five agencies listed in Item 2). See attached NCRIC ALPR Policy and CA DOJ Memorandum regarding SB34 and SB54
  11. Any policies or directives addressing NCRIC's obligations under California's sanctuary or data-privacy laws (including the California Values Act, SB 54, and Civil Code § 1798.90.51 et seq.) as they relate to the sharing of ALPR data with federal immigration enforcement agencies. See attached NCRIC ALPR Policy and CA DOJ Memorandum regarding SB34 and SB54
  12. NCRIC's current ALPR Usage and Privacy Policy, as required by California Civil Code § 1798.90.51, including data retention schedules. See attached NCRIC ALPR Policy
  13. All annual audit reports of NCRIC's ALPR program required by California Civil Code § 1798.90.53, from January 1, 2023, to the present. ALPR audits were conducted in September 2025, December 2025, and February 2026. All had no findings, including 100% compliance with no participation from federal partners or agencies outside California. Records from prior audits were unable to be located.
  14. Records identifying the current data retention period for ALPR data held by NCRIC, and any records reflecting changes to the retention period during the timeframe of this request. See attached NCRIC ALPR Policy. The current retention period is 30 days
  15. Records of any unauthorized access, data breaches, policy violations, or security incidents involving ALPR data held by NCRIC. No such records exist.
  16. Records of any complaints or inquiries received from members of the public, advocacy organizations, or elected officials regarding NCRIC's ALPR data practices. No such records exist.
  17. A list of all categories of "hot lists" (e.g., stolen vehicles, AMBER alerts, BOLO lists) currently integrated into or compared against NCRIC's ALPR database(s), including the source of each hot list. "Felony Vehicles" and "Stolen Vehicles" from the "California SVS"
  18. Any records describing the use of custom or locally generated hot lists, including the criteria or authorization process for creating such lists. No such records exist.
  19. The names and titles of NCRIC personnel responsible for administering, overseeing, or auditing the ALPR program. Brian Rodrigues, NCRIC Assistant Deputy Director
  20. Agendas, minutes, or summaries of any meetings of NCRIC's Privacy, Civil Rights, and Civil Liberties Advisory Committee (or equivalent oversight body) at which ALPR data practices were discussed, from January 1, 2023, to the present. See attached "Exec Board Meeting Agenda 2024-09-17" and "NC HIDTA NCRIC Policy Procedure Section 16 ALPR June 2025"


[cid:image001.png@01DCA809.99401CC0]
