title: Organized Retail Crime Task Force: Auror

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: Organized Retail Crime Task Force: Auror
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Records related to the Organized Retail Crime Theft Task Force announced on June 23, 2022, specifically concerning the implementation, use, and discussion of Auror's crime intelligence platform.

1. All contracts, MOUs, RFPs, or service agreements between the AG's Office and Auror Inc. (auror.co) dated June 23, 2022, to present
1a. Amendments, scope-of-work documents, or SLA agreements related to Auror's services
1b. Invoices, payment receipts, or procurement documents for Auror software/services

2. Budget allocations specific to Auror platform implementation
2a. Documentation of federal/state grants used to fund Auror-related expenditures

3. Training manuals, guides, or protocols for Auror platform use by task force members
3a. Video recordings of Auror software training sessions
3b. Email communications regarding training schedules or materials

4. All incident reports, intelligence briefings, or case files generated through Auror's platform
4a. Spreadsheets/databases tracking organized retail crime incidents processed via Auror
4b. Performance metrics/reports evaluating Auror's effectiveness (2022-2024)

5. Records of technical malfunctions, data inaccuracies, or security breaches involving Auror
5a. Formal complaints from task force members or partner agencies about Auror's system
5b. Internal audits or third-party assessments of Auror's compliance with state data laws

6. Emails/memos between AG staff and Auror representatives (June 23, 2022-present)
6a. Meeting minutes where Auror platform use was discussed
6b. Correspondence with federal partners (FBI, DHS) regarding Auror data sharing

These records are requested from June 23, 2022, to present; Electronic copies are preferred in their original formats wherever possible, and commonly readable formats such as pdf, mp4, png where conversion is required.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Robin Monks

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. communications.subject: PRR Closed - Monks - PRR-2025-0182
communications.communication:
Dear Robin Monks, Thank you for your email received in our office on June 6, 2025, asking about the status of this request. This correspondence is to confirm the closure of your public records request. You requested the following: Records related to the Organized Retail Crime Theft Task Force announced on June 23, 2022, specifically concerning the implementation, use, and discussion of Auror's crime intelligence platform. 
1. All contracts, MOUs, RFPs, or service agreements between the AG's Office and Auror Inc. (auror.co) dated June 23, 2022, to present 
1a. Amendments, scope-of-work documents, or SLA agreements related to Auror's services 
1b. Invoices, payment receipts, or procurement documents for Auror software/services 
2. Budget allocations specific to Auror platform implementation 
2a. Documentation of federal/state grants used to fund Auror-related expenditures 
3. Training manuals, guides, or protocols for Auror platform use by task force members 
3a. Video recordings of Auror software training sessions 
3b. Email communications regarding training schedules or materials 
4. All incident reports, intelligence briefings, or case files generated through Auror's platform 
4a. Spreadsheets/databases tracking organized retail crime incidents processed via Auror 
4b. Performance metrics/reports evaluating Auror's effectiveness (2022-2024) 
5. Records of technical malfunctions, data inaccuracies, or security breaches involving Auror 
5a. Formal complaints from task force members or partner agencies about Auror's system 
5b. Internal audits or third-party assessments of Auror's compliance with state data laws 
6. Emails/memos between AG staff and Auror representatives (June 23, 2022-present) 
6a. Meeting minutes where Auror platform use was discussed 
6b. Correspondence with federal partners (FBI, DHS) regarding Auror data sharing 
These records are requested from June 23, 2022, to present; Electronic copies are preferred in their original formats wherever possible, and commonly readable formats such as pdf, mp4, png where conversion is required. 
The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 
In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. We have searched our records and have not located any records responsive to your request. There are no records responsive to your request. Your request is now closed, and this is our final response. The Office of the Attorney General does not intend to further address the request, and the Public Records Act's one-year statute of limitations to seek judicial review has started to run as of the date of this correspondence. If you have any questions, please contact me within 30 days via email or at Office of the Attorney General, Public Records & Constituent Services, P.O. Box 40100, Olympia, WA 98504-0100. Thank you. Sincerely, 
 Kristin Young 
Kristin Young 
Senior Public Records Officer 
360-586-3568 
Kristin.Young@atg.wa.gov

1a. Amendments, scope-of-work documents, or SLA agreements related to Auror's services 
1b. Invoices, payment receipts, or procurement documents for Auror software/services 
2. Budget allocations specific to Auror platform implementation 
2a. Documentation of federal/state grants used to fund Auror-related expenditures 
3. Training manuals, guides, or protocols for Auror platform use by task force members 
3a. Video recordings of Auror software training sessions 
3b. Email communications regarding training schedules or materials 
4. All incident reports, intelligence briefings, or case files generated through Auror's platform 
4a. Spreadsheets/databases tracking organized retail crime incidents processed via Auror 
4b. Performance metrics/reports evaluating Auror's effectiveness (2022-2024) 
5. Records of technical malfunctions, data inaccuracies, or security breaches involving Auror 
5a. Formal complaints from task force members or partner agencies about Auror's system 
5b. Internal audits or third-party assessments of Auror's compliance with state data laws 
6. Emails/memos between AG staff and Auror representatives (June 23, 2022-present) 
6a. Meeting minutes where Auror platform use was discussed 
6b. Correspondence with federal partners (FBI, DHS) regarding Auror data sharing 
These records are requested from June 23, 2022, to present; Electronic copies are preferred in their original formats wherever possible, and commonly readable formats such as pdf, mp4, png where conversion is required. 
The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 
In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. An installment of records responsive to your request has been uploaded to Box. The records consist of a 133-page document (RM-01004 thru RM-01136) and 0 (MB) of native data. You may inspect and/or download these records via this link: https://atg-wa.box.com/s/sbdpifp3cbev8baytvjd8e0ip52alix1 . Please do so within 30 days. Records provided via Box are free of charge. You do not need a Box account to access the records. If a password is required to access the records, one will be provided to you in a separate email. Also uploaded with these responsive documents is a key to the codes indicated as black boxes with a code, e.g., [1a], centered. The key identifies the statutory basis for the exemption as well as a brief explanation for why the exemption applies. As you review the responsive documents, you will be able to refer to the key to identify the exemption and its application. We have made every attempt to apply consistent redactions on documents that contain the same information. In the event you locate documents that contain redactions that are not applied consistently, please let me know. If you prefer to receive these records on CD, USB thumb drive, or paper, there is a charge. Please see the AGO Public Records Fee Schedule for cost information. If you prefer one of these formats, please submit a check or money order to my attention within 30 days, in the amount specified on the Fee Schedule, payable to the Office of the Attorney General, and send it to the address below. Once payment is received, the records will be mailed. Please be advised that if you do not either download these records, access them for inspection, or submit payment for an alternate format on or before November 2, 2025, the request is considered to have been fulfilled and is closed on that date for failure to claim or review records. This means that the Attorney General’s Office will not further address the request, and that is the date from which the Public Records Act’s one-year statute of limitations to seek judicial review starts to run. Please contact me within 30 days via email or at Office of the Attorney General, Public Records & Constituent Services, P.O. Box 40100, Olympia, WA 98504-0100, if you have any questions. Thank you. Sincerely, Kristin Young

5a. Formal complaints from task force members or partner agencies about Auror's system 
5b. Internal audits or third-party assessments of Auror's compliance with state data laws 
6. Emails/memos between AG staff and Auror representatives (June 23, 2022-present) 
6a. Meeting minutes where Auror platform use was discussed 
6b. Correspondence with federal partners (FBI, DHS) regarding Auror data sharing 
These records are requested from June 23, 2022, to present; Electronic copies are preferred in their original formats wherever possible, and commonly readable formats such as pdf, mp4, png where conversion is required. 
The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 
In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. An installment of records responsive to your request has been uploaded to Box. The records consist of a 357-page document ( RM-01366 thru RM-01722) and 0 (MB) of native data. You may inspect and/or download these records via this link: https://atg-wa.box.com/s/qa589k16b82j5y0gy6jkwxsaee1xbukh . Please do so within 30 days. Records provided via Box are free of charge. You do not need a Box account to access the records. If a password is required to access the records, one will be provided to you in a separate email. Also uploaded with these responsive documents is a key to the codes indicated as black boxes with a code, e.g., [1a], centered. The key identifies the statutory basis for the exemption as well as a brief explanation for why the exemption applies. As you review the responsive documents, you will be able to refer to the key to identify the exemption and its application. We have made every attempt to apply consistent redactions on documents that contain the same information. In the event you locate documents that contain redactions that are not applied consistently, please let me know. If you prefer to receive these records on CD, USB thumb drive, or paper, there is a charge. Please see the AGO Public Records Fee Schedule for cost information. If you prefer one of these formats, please submit a check or money order to my attention within 30 days, in the amount specified on the Fee Schedule, payable to the Office of the Attorney General, and send it to the address below. Once payment is received, the records will be mailed. Please be advised that if you do not either download these records, access them for inspection, or submit payment for an alternate format on or before March 29, 2026, the request is considered to have been fulfilled and is closed on that date for failure to claim or review records. This means that the Attorney General’s Office will not further address the request, and that is the date from which the Public Records Act’s one-year statute of limitations to seek judicial review starts to run. Please contact me within 30 days via email or at Office of the Attorney General, Public Records & Constituent Services, P.O. Box 40100, Olympia, WA 98504-0100, if you have any questions. Thank you. Sincerely, Kristin Young 
Kristin Young 
Senior Public Records Officer 
360-586-3568 
Kristin.Young@atg.wa.gov

title: State of Virginia Salaries 2025 & 2026

communications.subject: Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: State of Virginia Salaries 2025 & 2026
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Virginia Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

State of Virginia 2025 Compensation Data for All Employees (This report can be measured for CY2025, FY2025, or on a Date in Time in 2025, whichever is readily available)
State of Virginia 2026 Compensation Data for All Employees (This report can be measured As of Today's Date)
Please provide the following fields for each employee in the dataset:
Employee # | First Name | Middle Name/Initial | Last Name | Employee Role Title | Position Number | Agency Name | Agency Location | Agency Address | Agency City/State/ZIP | Employee Category | Compensation (Base Pay, Salary, Hourly Rate, Total Pay, etc) | Overtime | Benefits Pay | Date Hired | Date Terminated | FT/PT

Note: If not all fields listed above are releasable as public record or available please disregard those individual fields for this request.

The following fields were provided in a request for 2024 data and the same fields for 2025 will satisfy this request:
Business Unit |	Company |	Agency Description | 	Last Name |	First Name |	Empl Class |	Employee Type |	Job Std Hours |	Position Description |	VPA Only |	Job Code |	Standard Occup Classification |	Salary Plan	Grade |	Step |	Reg/Temp |	Full- Part Time	 |State Comp Rate |	Non State Comp Rate |	Special Comp Rate |	Combined Comp Rate |	Hourly Comp Rate |	FTE |	Company Seniority Date |	Employee State Begin Date


I would prefer the request be filled electronically, by either e-mail attachment or FTP Download if available, otherwise sent through mail via CD/DVD. I would appreciate the request to be filled in an accessible format, including but not limited to: CSV, Excel, Microsoft Access database, or other bulk list file type.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Tom Nash, a citizen of Virginia, in coordination with Tyler King.

title: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Burlington County Sheriff)

communications.subject: New Jersey Open Public Records Act Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Burlington County Sheriff)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New Jersey Open Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields or dates is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 7 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe

title: Recruitment and Hiring (Austin Police Department)

communications.subject: Texas Public Information Act Request: Recruitment and Hiring (Austin Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Texas Public Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

I am requesting records related to the department's efforts to recruit and hire new officers:
Records include digital and physical, released in their native forms (Excel, PDF, etc)

- ad purchases
- contracts with vendors for ad placement, recruitment and hiring 
- market research and data, if collected
- brand and department strategies created by ad agencies or internally
- data related to applications, KPIs of recruitment and hiring campaigns
- PowerPoint or equivalent presentations, including accompanying documents related to recruitment, hiring, and marketing strategies
- Budgets for recruitment and hiring internally, and the total money spent 
- Pay bonuses or financial incentives to retain officers
- Data by year on the number of officers, sworn and non-sworn

Search from 2021 to today's date.
Please provide in rolling production

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Joey Scott

communications.subject: Public Information Act Request :: R158707-052225
communications.communication:
Dear Joey Scott: 
 
Thank you for your interest in the public records of the Austin Police Department. Your request has been received and is being processed. Your request was received in this office on 5/22/2025 and given the reference number R158707-052225 for tracking purposes. 
Please be advised that the Austin Police Department’s Central Records Division receives over 2500 requests for information a month. These requests are processed in the order received, and we strive to respond to each request promptly, as required by law. 
Please know that, as a result of the large volume of requests received, the anticipated timeframe for responding to your request may take 10 business days if it is simply an offense report you're requesting.  If you are requesting body cam, dash cam, or other related records your request could take up to 30 days. 
You will be contacted about the availability and/or provided with copies of the records requested. PLEASE NOTE: The Texas Public Information Act does not require a governmental body to create new information, to do legal research, or to answer questions. 
You can monitor the progress of your request at the link below and you’ll receive an email when your request has been completed. Again, thank you for using the Austin Public Records Center. 
Austin Police Department

title: Department of Defense support for upcoming film "Hotel Tehran"

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Department of Defense support for upcoming film "Hotel Tehran"
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Any documents pertaining to collaboration between the Department of Defense (DoD) and the production of upcoming movie "Hotel Tehran" directed by Guy Moshe. Such documents may include but should not be limited to: script notes; production assistance agreements; and all records of communications between employees of DoD and members of the cast, crew, and production company of "Hotel Tehran," including memos, notes of meetings and conference calls, emails, and other forms of communication.

"Hotel Tehran" appears on a list of DoD entertainment industry collaborations released to me through FOIA. The list is attached to this request.

I am the Director of Research Operations at a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization called The Decoding Culture Foundation as well as the editor-in-chief and a contributor to a research magazine published by said foundation titled "Cultural Engineering Studies." These documents will be used for public education and research purposes. I therefore request a waiver of all fees that may otherwise apply. Attached is a document with organization letterhead confirming my position, as well as the organization's 501(c)(3) application and approval form.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Brett Carollo

title: Department of Defense support for 2024 documentary series "Remembering D-Day: 80 Years Later"

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Department of Defense support for 2024 documentary series "Remembering D-Day: 80 Years Later"
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

All documents pertaining to collaboration between the Department of Defense (DoD) and the production of the 2024 documentary "Remembering D-Day: 80 Year Later." Such documents may include but should not be limited to: script notes; production assistance agreements; photographs; and all records of communications between employees of DoD and members of the cast, crew, and production company of "Remembering D-Day: 80 Years Later," including memos, notes of meetings and conference calls, emails, and other forms of communication.

"D-DAY+80" appears on a list of DoD entertainment industry collaborations released to me through FOIA. The list is attached to this request.

I am the Director of Research Operations at a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization called The Decoding Culture Foundation as well as the editor-in-chief and a contributor to a research magazine published by said foundation titled "Cultural Engineering Studies." These documents will be used for public education and research purposes. I therefore request a waiver of all fees that may otherwise apply. Attached is a document with organization letterhead confirming my position, as well as the organization's 501(c)(3) application and approval form.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Brett Carollo

title: Open Records Requst: ALPR Audit

communications.subject: Georgia Open Records Act Request: Open Records Requst: ALPR Audit
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. Organization Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of June 1, 2024, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Organization Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches done within the agency.

2. Network Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of June 1, 2024, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Network Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches of the organization’s Flock network by any agency in the Flock System.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

e

communications.subject: Flock Audit Requests
communications.communication:
Good morning,

The Dublin Police Department has received your request for Flock audit for June 1,2024 to May 28, 2025. Due to the amount of data needing to be recovered and formatted there will be a copying/preparation fee of $21.44 per hour and a fee of $0.40 per disc due to the reports being too large to send via email. A shipping fee will also be required. I cannot give you an estimated total until I complete the request. Please advise if you wish to continue. Also a copy of our payment worksheet is attached for further clarification.

Thank you


[cid:image003.png@01DBD91E.CB30A250]

Files that were recently shared with you on Dropbox are saved for easy access - open them any time to review. If you have edit access, you can also comment, download, edit, or share again. communications.subject: (2/9) Sharing is caring
communications.communication:
Learn how to share with Dropbox ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ 
‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌

<https://www.dropbox.com/?oref=e&_tk=email&_camp=8862324&_ad=d11210dd5d7247ffbd1c284486fd3446&utm_source=iter&utm_medium=email&utm_term=us-en&utm_campaign=p1_bb_co_all_pcn_b202111&utm_content=h_l>

Share any file or folder easily, with anyone 
By just sharing a link, you can send anything – from Office Docs and PDFs to 
zipped folders and large CAD files – to anyone, even if they don’t have a 
Dropbox account. Add password protection and expiration dates to ensure only 
the right people see your files.*

Big needs? With Dropbox Transfer, you can send large files up to 100 GB 
without taking up space in your Dropbox account.** 





Learn More 
<https://learn.dropbox.com/self-guided-learning/dropbox-fundamentals-course/how-to-use-dropbox#how-to-share-a-file-or-folder-from-your-dropbox-account>
 
<https://learn.dropbox.com/self-guided-learning/dropbox-fundamentals-course/how-to-use-dropbox#how-to-share-a-file-or-folder-from-your-dropbox-account>




Share what you want, how you want 
You can save, access, and share your important stuff from anywhere, at any 
time, on any device. 

Start sharing <https://www.dropbox.com/work?file_chooser_type=sharing> 

 <https://www.dropbox.com/work?file_chooser_type=sharing> 



All of your large file transfer needs covered 
Dropbox Transfer lets you send up to 2 GB per transfer with your Dropbox Basic 
plan, up to 100 GB with Dropbox Essentials and Dropbox Business and up to 250 
GB with Dropbox Business Plus. Send videos, project files, and track downloads. 

Create a transfer <https://www.dropbox.com/transfer> 

 <https://www.dropbox.com/transfer> 

*Some features require upgrading your account to a paid Dropbox plan.
Learn how and view plans. <https://www.dropbox.com/plans> 
**The size limit of your transfer depends on the plan you have. Click for more 
information. <https://help.dropbox.com/share/dropbox-transfer>

Quick and Easy 

Save, view, share and access the files and folders stored in your Dropbox 
account. 
How to add files to Dropbox: 
 * Log in <https://www.dropbox.com/login> to Dropbox.com <>. 
 * Click Upload under the search bar at the top. 
 * Click Files or Folder: 
 * If you choose Files, select as many files as you like and click Open. 
 * If you choose Folder, select a folder and click Upload. 
 * Alternatively, use the drag and drop option to upload files.

title: Audit letters of compliance for Danbury, CT 2018-2024

communications.subject: Connecticut Freedom of Information Act Request: Audit letters of compliance for Danbury, CT 2018-2024
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Connecticut Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. Management Letters: All final "Management Letters" or "Letters to Those Charged with Governance" issued by the City’s external auditors (including but not limited to CliftonLarsonAllen LLP) for the fiscal years ending 2018 through 2025.

2. Internal Control Deficiencies: Any written communications from external auditors regarding "significant deficiencies" or "material weaknesses" in the City’s internal controls related to departmental compliance with the City Charter, specifically regarding the qualification of officers under Charter Section 2-5.

3. Compliance Testing Records: Any audit "working papers" or summaries in the City's possession that document the testing of Oaths of Office for the Health Department Directors (Fernanda Carvalho, Kara Prunty, and Lisa Morrissey).

4. Single Audit Compliance: For the years 2018–2024, please provide the "Report on Compliance for Each Major State/Federal Program" that specifically addresses the Health Department’s adherence to administrative legal requirements.

Public Interest & Urgency: These records are of vital public interest following the $17 million verdict in Girolametti v. Danbury, which established a pattern of "reckless disregard" within City departments. The failure of three consecutive Health Directors to file mandatory Oaths of Office suggests a systemic failure of internal controls that the auditors were tasked to identify.

I request a response within the statutory four business days. If any portion of this request is denied, please cite the specific exemption and provide the name of the official responsible for the denial.

I also request that, if possible and pursuant to Conn. Gen. Stat. § 1-212(d), fees be waived as I believe this request is in the public interest and not made for commercial gain. The requested documents will be made available to the public at MuckRock.com.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 4 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Kevin Paige

communications.communication:
This request is now in a state of "Deemed Denial" under CGS § 1-206(a), as more than four business days have passed without a production of records or a valid legal explanation for their withholding.

Please be advised that per the Connecticut Freedom of Information Commission's strict 30-day jurisdictional window, I will be filing a formal Notice of Appeal if these records are not received by February 27, 2026. I would prefer to resolve this via this portal, but I must act to preserve my state-mandated appeal rights.

title: Request log, record retention schedule and forms

communications.subject: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request: Request log, record retention schedule and forms
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

- all record retention schedules
- master list of forms
- a log of all requests submitted to the Massachusetts State Athletic Commission, from the earliest available date until this request is fulfilled. Please include the date of request, the requester's name, the requester's organizational affiliation, a description of the request and the date the request was filled.

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as we believe this request is in the public interest, as suggested but not stipulated by the recommendations of the Massachusetts Supervisor of Public Records. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, and is not made for commercial usage.

I expect the request to be filled in an accessible format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical documents.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Chris Amico

communications.subject: FW: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request: Request log, record retention schedule and forms
communications.communication:
Hi Chris,
We received your public records request and have questions before we are able to fulfill. Can you help specify:
- master list of forms
- a log of all requests submitted to the Massachusetts State Athletic Commission, from the earliest available date until this request is fulfilled.

We are not sure what you mean by "master list of forms" and for "log of all requests" are you referring to public records requests?

Thank you.
Cheers,
Michele

Michele J. Campbell
Director of Communications
Office of Consumer Affairs and Business Regulation
1 Federal Street, Suite 720
Boston, MA 02110-2012

communications.subject: RE: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request: Request log, record retention schedule and forms
communications.communication:
Hi--

Thanks for the quick reply. Yes, I meant a log of public records requests. Sorry if that was unclear. 

For the master list of forms, I'm looking for something like this form that the California State Athletic Commission produces: https://www.documentcloud.org/documents/26050334-csac_forms_tracking/. I don't know if there's a better term for it.

Very much appreciate your help. Please let me know if you have other questions.

Best,
Chris

title: Polymarket

communications.subject: New York Freedom of Information Law Request: Polymarket
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:
- Copies of all inquiries/communications.complaints to the NY Office of the Attorney General mentioning or regarding "Polymarket" or "Poly Market" submitted between June 1, 2020 and the time this request is processed.

I am a news reporter for WIRED (wired.com), so the requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. Therefore, I request that any fees are waived. But in the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available.

I am also asking for my request to be expedited. The documents I am requesting are newsworthy, given the current news-cycle concerning Polymarket as it relates to bets on Venezuela:
https://www.wsj.com/world/americas/maduro-polymarket-bet-a2e5d100
https://www.forbes.com/sites/siladityaray/2026/01/07/why-polymarket-is-not-paying-bets-on-the-us-invading-venezuela/

If you need to contact me, you can reach me at caroline_haskins@wired.com, or here. Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Caroline

communications.subject: [NYS OAG FOIL Request Center] General FOIL Request :: G000017-010726
communications.communication:
February 27, 2026 RE:  Freedom of Information Law (FOIL) Request Reference # G000017-010726 Dear Caroline Haskins: This email is in response to the above-­referenced FOIL request. The Office of the Attorney General (“OAG”) has conducted a diligent search for the records that you have requested. Please access this request in the NYS OAG FOIL Request Center to retrieve records that respond to your request.  Please be advised that some of the records that respond to your request have been withheld in their entirety, or portions have been redacted and appear as blacked out in the documents produced to you.  These records, or portions of records, are exempt from disclosure pursuant to: • Public Officers Law § 87(2)(b), because their disclosure would constitute an unwarranted invasion of personal privacy; • Public Officers Law § 87(2)(e), because the documents requested were compiled for law-enforcement purposes and would, if disclosed, interfere with law-enforcement investigations or judicial proceedings; and • Public Officers Law § 87(2)(g), because the records are inter-agency or intra-agency materials. 
You have a right to appeal the foregoing decision.  If you elect to do so, you must appeal in writing, within 30 days, to Kathryn Sheingold, Records Appeals Officer.  Your appeal can be sent by email to FOIL.Appeals@ag.ny.gov or by mail to State of New York, Office of the Attorney General, Division of Appeals and Opinions, The Capitol, Albany, New York 12224.  You can reach the Records Appeals Officer by telephone at (518) 776-2009. Please note that records, or portions thereof, that respond to your request are drawn from the opinions, statements, or other content provided by a complainant and do not necessarily reflect the views of the OAG.  You should not construe any information provided to you by this Office to imply the Attorney General’s endorsement, recommendation, or disapproval of a given entity. 

Any numerical or other data provided by the OAG is compiled by sources believed to be reliable.  However, because of the dynamic nature of this Office’s receipt and processing of complaint information, we cannot guarantee that this information is perfect. 

When you review complaint information, you may wish to consider the entity’s size and its volume of business transactions.  You should also note the nature of the complaints and an entity’s responses to them, because that information may be equally, if not more, important than the number of complaints received by the OAG. 
This is our second and final response to the above-referenced request. Accordingly, this FOIL request is now closed. Sincerely, Records Access Officer 
 
Please visit the NYS OAG FOIL Request Center to view frequently asked questions, monitor request progress, and submit future requests. 
 View this request in the My Request Center.

title: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Tangipahoa Parish Sheriff's Office)

communications.subject: Louisiana Public Records Law Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Tangipahoa Parish Sheriff's Office)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Louisiana Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields or dates is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe

title: Illinois HHS response

communications.subject: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: Illinois HHS response
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records:

Communications and correspondence with the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services regarding the Aug. 26, 2025, HHS letter requesting the Illinois Department of Human Services remove content from the state's Personal Responsibility Education Program curricula and program materials. For reference, the letter can be found here: https://acf.gov/sites/default/files/documents/main/08.26.25-Combined-PREP-Letters.pdf.

I also request internal communications between any Illinois Department of Human Services employees about the aforementioned letter. If the content changes were made, I request a copy of the material before it was changed as well as a copy of the material after it was changed.

I additionally request that, if possible, fees be waived as I believe this request is in the public interest. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, processed by a representative of the news media/press and is made in the process of news gathering and not for commercial usage.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Ileana Garnand

communications.subject: 26-FTS-8529 Garnand - Extension
communications.communication:
Dear Illeana Garnand:

The Illinois Department of Human Services (IDHS) received your request for records pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) on January 12, 2026.  Your request seeks the following:


                Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records:

Communications and correspondence with the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services regarding the Aug. 26, 2025, HHS letter requesting the Illinois Department of Human Services remove content from the state's Personal Responsibility Education Program curricula and program materials. For reference, the letter can be found here: https://acf.gov/sites/default/files/documents/main/08.26.25-Combined-PREP-Letters.pdf.

I also request internal communications between any Illinois Department of Human Services employees about the aforementioned letter. If the content changes were made, I request a copy of the material before it was changed as well as a copy of the material after it was changed.
Under the Freedom of Information Act, a public body may extend the time to respond to a FOIA request by up to five business days. 5 ILCS 140/3(e).  As such, the Department is extending the time to respond to your request by five business days on one or more of the following bases:


  *   The requested records are stored in whole or in part at other locations than the office having charge of the requested records;
  *   The request requires the collection of a substantial number of specified records;
  *   The requested records have not been located in the course of a routine search and additional efforts are being made to locate them;
  *   The requested records require examination and evaluation by personnel having the necessary competence and discretion to determine if they are exempt from disclosure or should be revealed only with appropriate deletions; and
  *   The request for records cannot be complied with by the public body within the time limits prescribed by FOIA without unduly burdening or interfering with the operation of the public body.

The Department will respond to your request on or before January 27, 2026.  Thank you for your patience and interest in the Illinois Department of Human Services.

Sincerely,

Sean Reddington (he/him/his)
Associate General Counsel | Office of the General Counsel
Illinois Department of Human Services
Email: DHS.FOIAOfficer@illinois.gov<mailto:DHS.FOIAOfficer@illinois.gov>

[IDHS Illinois Department of Human Services]



State of Illinois - CONFIDENTIALITY NOTICE: The information contained in this communication is confidential, may be attorney-client privileged or attorney work product, may constitute inside information or internal deliberative staff communication, and is intended only for the use of the addressee. Unauthorized use, disclosure or copying of this communication or any part thereof is strictly prohibited and may be unlawful. If you have received this communication in error, please notify the sender immediately by return e-mail and destroy this communication and all copies thereof, including all attachments. Receipt by an unintended recipient does not waive attorney-client privilege, attorney work product privilege, or any other exemption from disclosure.

communications.subject: 26-FTS-8529 Garnand Partial Response and extension request
communications.communication:
Dear Ileana Garnand:

The Illinois Department of Human Services (IDHS) received your request for records pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) on January 12, 2026.  Your request seeks the following:


                Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records:

  1.  Communications and correspondence with the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services regarding the Aug. 26, 2025, HHS letter requesting the Illinois Department of Human Services remove content from the state's Personal Responsibility Education Program curricula and program materials. For reference, the letter can be found here: https://acf.gov/sites/default/files/documents/main/08.26.25-Combined-PREP-Letters.pdf.
  2.  I also request internal communications between any Illinois Department of Human Services employees about the aforementioned letter. If the content changes were made, I request a copy of the material before it was changed as well as a copy of the material after it was changed.

The Department is still processing Item 1 of your request. Accordingly, the Department is requesting an additional five business day extension, which is permissible under Section 3(e) of FOIA.  5 ILCS 140/3(e).  Under the new deadline, the Department will respond on or before February 3, 2026 .  If you do not agree to this extension, please contact me at your earliest convenience.

Regarding Item 2, a search for electronic records of internal IDHS communications relating to the "Personal Responsibility Education Program" from the date of the HHS letter to the date of your request returned over 900 responsive records. To fulfill your request, as written, the Department would have to convert all correspondence files and their attachments to an appropriate format, review them carefully for content, and apply redactions where necessary prior to release.  This process would take approximately 80 work hours, and it would significantly interfere with the operations of the Department.  As a result, the Department has determined that your request is overly broad and unduly burdensome. 5 ILCS 140/3(g).  Please feel free to submit a revised request with additional information, including specific dates, key words, or email accounts which would allow us to refine this search to a manageable level.

To the extent you consider any portion of this response an improper denial of your FOIA request, you have a right to seek review by writing to the Public Access Counselor (PAC) at the following address:

Public Access Counselor
Office of the Attorney General
500 South 2nd Street
Springfield, Illinois 62706
Fax: 217-782-1396
E-Mail: Public.Access@ilag.gov<mailto:Public.Access@ilag.gov>

If you choose to file a Request for Review with the PAC, you must do so within 60 calendar days of the date of this letter. 5 ILCS 140/9.5(a). Please note that you must include a copy of your original FOIA request and this response when filing a Request for Review with the PAC. You also have the right to seek judicial review of this response. 5 ILCS 140/11.

Thank you for your interest in the Illinois Department of Human Services.

Sincerely,

Sean Reddington (he/him/his)
Associate General Counsel | Office of the General Counsel
Illinois Department of Human Services
Email: DHS.FOIAOfficer@illinois.gov<mailto:DHS.FOIAOfficer@illinois.gov>

[IDHS Illinois Department of Human Services]

Unauthorized use, disclosure or copying of this communication or any part thereof is strictly prohibited and may be unlawful. If you have received this communication in error, please notify the sender immediately by return e-mail and destroy this communication and all copies thereof, including all attachments. Receipt by an unintended recipient does not waive attorney-client privilege, attorney work product privilege, or any other exemption from disclosure.

title: Department of Defense support for "Fox NFL Sunday Veteran's Day Weekend Special"

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Department of Defense support for "Fox NFL Sunday Veteran's Day Weekend Special"
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

All documents pertaining to collaboration between the Department of Defense (DoD) and the production of the "Fox NFL Sunday Veteran's Day Weekend Special" in 2024. Such documents may include but should not be limited to: script notes; production assistance agreements; photographs; and all records of communications between employees of DoD and members of the cast, crew, and production company of "Fox NFL Sunday Veteran's Day Weekend Special," including memos, notes of meetings and conference calls, emails, and other forms of communication.

"Fox NFL Sunday Veteran's Day Weekend Special" appears on a list of DoD entertainment industry collaborations released to me through FOIA. The list is attached to this request.

I am the Director of Research Operations at a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization called The Decoding Culture Foundation as well as the editor-in-chief and a contributor to a research magazine published by said foundation titled "Cultural Engineering Studies." These documents will be used for public education and research purposes. I therefore request a waiver of all fees that may otherwise apply. Attached is a document with organization letterhead confirming my position, as well as the organization's 501(c)(3) application and approval form.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Brett Carollo

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Michael Ryan Davis (aka Mike Davis) (Federal Bureau of Investigation)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

All files on Michael Ryan Davis, professionally known as Mike Davis, a political activist, writer, and historian who wrote frequently about California. Davis lived March 10, 1946 – October 25, 2022, and his death was widely reported in the media.

https://www.npr.org/2022/10/26/1131578207/writer-truck-driver-meatcutter-and-prophet-of-compassion-mike-davis-dies-at-76

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jacob Silverman

communications.subject: RE: [EXTERNAL EMAIL] - RE: Freedom of Information Act Request: Michael Ryan Davis (aka Mike Davis) (Federal Bureau of Investigation)
communications.communication:
Thank you for contacting foipaquestions@fbi.gov.  Please check the status of your FOIPA Request at http://vault.fbi.gov by clicking on “Check Status of Your FOI/PA Request” on the right side of the page, and follow the instructions below.

Check the Status of Your FOIPA Request
If your FOIPA Number is [1234567-0] please enter [1234567-000] into the system. If your FOIPA Number is [1234567-1] please enter [1234567-001] into the system. If you have any questions about the status of your FOIPA request, please e-mail foipaquestions@fbi.gov.

FIND STATUS OF FOIPA- Request statuses are updated weekly

Please enter the whole FOIPA number-Example: [1234567-000]

FOIPA:
Results will show the Request Number, Case Type and Process Description shown below:
FOIPA:
1234567-000

Case Type:
FOIPA

Process Description (Will display the current progress of the request)
The FBI’s FOIPA Program is searching the FBI’s indices for potentially responsive documents.
You may be contacted via formal letter for all fees and/or negotiation issues that may apply.

NOTE: Recent requests are entered into the FOIPA database in the order that they are received. Before you can check the status, you must have received correspondence assigning a FOIPA request number and the information transferred to the online database. Status information is updated weekly. If a request has been closed within the last six months the online database will display the following: The FOIPA number entered has been closed, and appropriate correspondence has been sent to the address on file.

Estimated Dates of Completion
Requests are processed in the order in which they are received through our multi-track processing system.  Requests are divided into two primary tracks--simple (under 50 pages of potentially responsive documents) and complex (over 50 pages of potentially responsive documents).  Complex requests are further divided into medium, large, and extra-large sub-tracks based upon request size.  Simple track requests typically require the least amount of time to process.  Currently, simple track cases average approximately 122 days from the date of receipt for processing.  Our complex requests in the medium processing track are currently averaging 1,230 days, large processing track are currently averaging approximately 2,010 days, and extra-large processing track are currently averaging 2,370 days for processing.

Respectfully,

Public Information Officer
FBI - Information Management Division
200 Constitution Drive
Winchester, VA  22602
O: (540) 868-4593
E: foipaquestions@fbi.gov<mailto:foipaquestions@fbi.gov>

Do you have further questions about the FOI/PA process? Visit us at http://www.fbi.gov/foia

Please check the status of your request online at https://vault.fbi.gov/fdps-1/@@search-fdps    Status updates are performed on a weekly basis.

Note:  This is a non-emergency email address.  If this is an emergency, please call 911 directly.  If you need to report a tip for immediate action, please contact FBI Tips at http://tips.fbi.gov/ or reach out to your local field office.

title: Ivan Illich (Central Intelligence Agency)

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Ivan Illich (Central Intelligence Agency)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Records relating to or mentioning Ivan Illich AKA Ivan Ilich (4 September 1926 – 2 December 2002), a Croatian-Austrian philosopher, Roman Catholic priest, and critic of the institutions of modern Western culture, who addressed contemporary practices in education, medicine, work, energy use, transportation, and economic development. Monsignor Ilich, of the New York Archdiocese, was accused of spreading Communism within the Church in South America. Cidoc operated from 1966 to 1976. In 1967 Illich severed his links with the Roman Catholic church. The church summoned him after a report by the CIA, but it was reportedly mostly concerned about the readership of pieces on the church. 

I am a member of the news media and request classification as such. I have previously written about the government and its activities, with some reaching over 100,000 readers. As such, as I have a reasonable expectation of publication and my editorial and writing skills are well established. In addition, I discuss and comment on the files online and make them available through non-profits such as the Internet Archive and MuckRock, disseminating them to a large audience. While my research is not limited to this, a great deal of it, including this, focuses on the activities and attitudes of the government itself. As such, it is not necessary for me to demonstrate the relevance of this particular subject in advance.

Additionally, case law states that “proof of the ability to disseminate the released information to a broad cross- section of the public is not required.” Judicial Watch, Inc. v. Dep’t of Justice, 365 F.3d 1108, 1126 (D.C. Cir. 2004); see Carney v. U.S. Dep’t of Justice, 19 F.3d 807, 814-15 (2d Cir. 1994). Further, courts have held that "qualified because it also had “firm” plans to “publish a number of . . . ‘document sets’” concerning United States foreign and national security policy." Under this criteria, as well, I qualify as a member of the news media.

Additionally, courts have held that the news media status "focuses on the nature of the requester, not its request. The provision requires that the request be “made by” a representative of the news media. Id. § 552(a)(4)(A)(ii)(II). A newspaper reporter, for example, is a representative of the news media regardless of how much interest there is in the story for which he or she is requesting information." As such, the details of the request itself are moot for the purposes of determining the appropriate fee category.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Emma Best

title: Carlos Federico Diaz-Alejandro

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Carlos Federico Diaz-Alejandro
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Any and all files on Cuban-American economist Carlos Federico Diaz-Alejandro (July 18, 1937 – July 17, 1985). He sometimes appeared under the names Carlos F. Diaz-Alejandro or Carlos Federico Diaz Alejandro as well. His death is widely-known and widely reported; as such, privacy concerns under FOIA Exemptions 6 and 7 are voided, as are any concerns under the Privacy Act. For example, you may find his New York Times obituary at the following link: https://www.nytimes.com/1985/07/20/nyregion/prof-carlos-diaz-alejandro-expert-on-latin-economics.html

If any potentially responsive records have been destroyed and/or transferred to NARA, then I request copies of the destruction or transfer slips as well as any other documentation relating to, mentioning or describing said transfer or destruction, to include but not be limited to confirmation that your agency has no other copies of said records. If previously released records are available, then I request a rolling release consisting of those records while additional records are located and processed for release.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Matthew Petti

communications.subject: RE: Freedom of Information Act Request #NRC2024138265
communications.communication:
To whom this may concern,

I hope this message finds you well. My name is Matthew Petti and I am writing in regards to my FOIA request NRC2024138265, for records related to Cuban-American economist Carlos Federico Diaz-Alejandro. On March 22, 2024, the Freedom of Information Act & Privacy Act Unit responded with a total denial, arguing that records on Diaz-Alejandro are exempt under the (b)(6) privacy exemption. "To obtain these records your request must demonstrate proof that the subject(s) of your request is deceased," wrote Acting Chief FOIA Officer Jarrod T. Panter.

Diaz-Alejandro passed away in 1985, so privacy concerns under FOIA Exemptions 6 and 7 are voided, as are any concerns under the Privacy Act. In my initial request, I included a link to the New York Times obituary for Diaz-Alejandro, which is also attached below. (https://www.nytimes.com/1985/07/20/nyregion/prof-carlos-diaz-alejandro-expert-on-latin-economics.html) Furthermore, I am attaching a recent article from the Yale Economic Growth Center on Diaz-Alejandro's intellectual legacy, which clearly mentions that he died. Therefore, I am appealing the Freedom of Information Act & Privacy Act Unit's decision to deny these records.

I look forward to hearing back your determination soon.

Sincerely,
Matthew Petti

title: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Yakima County Sheriff's Office)

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Yakima County Sheriff's Office)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields or dates is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe

title: Records request Anduril Arsenal-1 sewer and water lines/ Inkstick Media

communications.subject: Ohio Open Records Law Request: Records request Anduril Arsenal-1 sewer and water lines/ Inkstick Media
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Under the Ohio Open Records Law, §149.43 et seq., I hereby request the following records:

Copies of records submitted by the Verdantas civil engineering firm regarding utilities serving the Arsenal-1 Hyperscale Manufacturing Facility in Pickaway County. Specifically, I am seeking all applications and construction plans for an extension of a water line and sanitary/sewer systems to serve the Anduril plant, including a proposed timeline for construction.

At a Madison Township Trustees zoning hearing in December, Amanda Spencer, the director of land development at Verdantas, described the planned utilities, saying: “As far as utilities go, we are planning an extension of a water line that will run along the roadway. It is tied in to an existing water line that was constructed with previous phases of development. It will head north on Airbase road, and it will head east on Airbase road. Eventually, it will continue south on Walnut Creek Pike and end at the southeast corner of the development. The sanitary will tie into a long planned sanitary extension by the City of Columbus that will be constructed on the south side of Airbase road, right along the top of this development.”

I first submitted this request to the Pickaway County Engineer, whose office told me to direct records requests related to water lines serving the plant to the Earnhart Hill Regional Water & Sewer District.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

I would request a prompt response to this request.  If you expect a significant delay in responding to or in fulfilling this request, please contact me with information about when I might expect copies of the requested records.

Sincerely,

Taylor Barnes, Inkstick Media

communications.communication:
Hello,

Thank you very much for providing me with the draft public water line project  for the Rickenbacker Parkway Waterline Extension 2025. 

Could you kindly confirm that, in the time since your last email, that you all have not received any application from Verdantas for water line plans for Arsenal-1? Has the firm submitted copies of all necessary shop drawings for material to be used on the project, copies of all necessary county or state highway permits, proof of insurance coverage for liability & property damage in the amounts required by the project specifications, and/or a valid State of Ohio, Bureau of Workers’ Compensation certificate? Does the Arsenal-1 developer have a "permit to install" from Earnhart Hill Regional Water & Sewer District? 

Thank you very much. I really appreciate the information so that I can get a sense of the timeline for this waterline extension being constructed.

best

Taylor Barnes

title: New York Freedom of Information Law Request: Drug Free Zone Sentence Enhancements: Department of Correction, NY

communications.subject: New York Freedom of Information Law Request: New York Freedom of Information Law Request: Drug Free Zone Sentence Enhancements: Department of Correction, NY
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New York Freedom of Information Law, I hereby request the following records:

A copy of all records related to persons serving or who have served a sentence for a state drug free zone law violation – under Consolidated Laws of New York, Penal 220.34. Specifically, I am requesting a list of all people currently or formerly incarcerated for a drug-free zone violation from 2015 to 6/30/2024. The list should include names, date of conviction, date of end of sentence, offenses convicted of, inmate ID, facility type (ie. state prison, local jail) and parole eligibility date.I would also like to request any demographic information that may be readily available for the above persons, such as, but not limited to, each person’s date of birth, age at arrest, current age, race, ethnicity and gender. If this time frame is overly burdensome, please provide a date range that can be fulfilled.
In addition, I am requesting a copy of any and all reports or studies generated by the state that discuss the passage and efficacy of the drug free zone law.
If there are any fees for obtaining or copying these records, please inform me should the cost exceed $100. However, I would also like to request a waiver of all fees as I am requesting the information on behalf of a 501(c)(3) nonprofit and the disclosure of the requested information is in the public interest and will contribute significantly to the public’s understanding of drug free zone laws. This information is not being sought for commercial purposes.
If possible, please provide the requested documents electronically in Excel format. However, if this is not feasible, please inform me of the available methods for obtaining these records. Further, if you expect a significant delay in responding to this request, please contact me with information concerning when I might expect copies or the ability to inspect the requested records.
If you deny any or all of this request, please cite each specific exemption that justifies the refusal to release the information and notify me of the appeal procedures available to me under the law.
Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Angel Carter

RE: FOIL Records Request of September 16, 2025, Reference # R004654-091625 
Dear Angel Carter, 
The NYS Department of Corrections and Community Supervision received a FOIL request from you on September 16, 2025. You requested: "To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the New York Freedom of Information Law, I hereby request the following records: 

A copy of all records related to persons serving or who have served a sentence for a state drug free zone law violation – under Consolidated Laws of New York, Penal 220.34. Specifically, I am requesting a list of all people currently or formerly incarcerated for a drug-free zone violation from 2015 to 6/30/2024. The list should include names, date of conviction, date of end of sentence, offenses convicted of, inmate ID, facility type (ie. state prison, local jail) and parole eligibility date.I would also like to request any demographic information that may be readily available for the above persons, such as, but not limited to, each person’s date of birth, age at arrest, current age, race, ethnicity and gender. If this time frame is overly burdensome, please provide a date range that can be fulfilled. 
In addition, I am requesting a copy of any and all reports or studies generated by the state that discuss the passage and efficacy of the drug free zone law. 
If there are any fees for obtaining or copying these records, please inform me should the cost exceed $100. However, I would also like to request a waiver of all fees as I am requesting the information on behalf of a 501(c)(3) nonprofit and the disclosure of the requested information is in the public interest and will contribute significantly to the public’s understanding of drug free zone laws. This information is not being sought for commercial purposes. 
If possible, please provide the requested documents electronically in Excel format. However, if this is not feasible, please inform me of the available methods for obtaining these records. Further, if you expect a significant delay in responding to this request, please contact me with information concerning when I might expect copies or the ability to inspect the requested records. 
If you deny any or all of this request, please cite each specific exemption that justifies the refusal to release the information and notify me of the appeal procedures available to me under the law. 
Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Angel Carter

title: Public Records Request: ReadyOp

communications.subject: North Carolina Public Records Law Request: Public Records Request: ReadyOp
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the North Carolina Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

- All contracts, licenses, MOUs, MOAs, data agreements, letters of intent, State of Work documents, Scope of Work documents, and other agreement documents with ReadyOp Communications that were active any time between 1/1/2023 and 2/4/2026.

- All financial documents or records of financial transactions, exchanges or obligations related to ReadyOp Communications dated between 1/1/2023 and 2/4/2026. Responsive materials include all invoices, bills, receipts, check stubs, or any other records, including those possessed in an electronic format.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

e
